Kuwaiti Armed Forces intercepted ballistic missiles and hostile drones inside Kuwaiti airspace since dawn, per Defense Ministry spokesman Col. Saud Abdulaziz Al-Atwan. He added that the Iranian aggression continued targeting several... #Kuwait 🇰🇼

RT by @lookner: Statement No. (76) The official spokesman for the Ministry of Defense, Colonel Saud Abdulaziz Al-Atwan, stated that the armed forces have detected, since dawn today, ballistic missiles and enemy drones, within Kuwaiti airspace, and they were intercepted and dealt with. He added that the sinful Iranian aggression continued to target a number of military and security installations, in addition to a number of vital and civilian facilities in the country, as the attacks targeted facilities belonging to the oil, electricity and water sectors, which led to the outbreak of fires and caused serious damage to a number of facilities and installations. He pointed out that the competent authorities began control and repair work, and this resulted in the injury of a number of General Fire Force men and a number of workers in the oil sector while they were carrying out their work, and they are receiving the necessary medical care and treatment. Operations to intercept hostile attacks also resulted in shrapnel falling on a number of sites and residential areas, resulting in material damage, without recording any human casualties, praise be to God. The Armed Forces confirm that they will continue to carry out their tasks and duties with high efficiency, within the framework of continuous preparedness and preparedness, and to take all necessary measures to protect the sovereignty of the nation and maintain its security and stability, in coordination with the competent authorities, in a way that preserves the safety of citizens and residents. #Kuwaiti_Army

RT by @lookner: This is yet another indication of how continued military escalation makes it increasingly difficult to prevent a wider regional conflict. Every U.S. escalation is met by an Iranian escalation, as each side seeks to establish new deterrence thresholds with every exchange. This is why the assumption that a broader military campaign will force Iran back to the negotiating table in a weaker position is far from guaranteed. An equally plausible outcome is a conflict that spirals beyond either side's intentions, expanding into a much broader regional war with diminishing opportunities to restore diplomacy. The central danger is not simply the use of military force, but the risk that escalation develops its own momentum. Once both sides become committed to demonstrating resolve and preserving deterrence, the political space for compromise narrows considerably, making a return to negotiations far more difficult than many in Washington may assume. #IranWar‌

Due to a combination of algorithm changes, rewrites to engagement weight and focus shifts from the “creator” program, it is unlikely that I will receive further biweekly payouts. So I will be getting rid of the blue checkmark. In 6 months, I was able to donate $1,510.70 simply from posting on here. I’m quite happy with this result. I was anticipating $500 by this time this year. If they mess with the payout system again I might consider rejoining the program. Thanks all for interacting with my posts! Instead of just giving some stranger a dopamine rush, you turned online “engagements” into real world impact!
